info.title: Slack Incoming Webhook
info.description:
Post messages to a Slack channel via an Incoming Webhook URL. The webhook URL itself authenticates — there's no Authorization header. URL pattern: `https://hooks.slack.com/services/<TEAM>/<WEBHOOK>/<TOKEN>`. Store the full URL in the vault and let the wrapper workflow split it into the 3 path segments before calling.

Response on success: HTTP 200 with body `ok`. On failure: HTTP 4xx with body describing the issue (`no_service`, `invalid_payload`, `channel_disabled`, etc.).

post.summary: Post a message to the channel bound to this webhook
post.description: Authentication is positional — the URL itself carries the secret. Path takes three segments: team_id (TXXXXXXXX), webhook_id (BXXXXXXXX), token (24-char random). Body accepts either a plain `text` string OR Block Kit `blocks` array (richer formatting).

schema.description: Either `text` alone, OR `blocks` (Block Kit). When both are present, `blocks` wins and `text` becomes the fallback shown in notifications / on clients that can't render blocks.

text.example: :bar_chart: Weekly token report ready — see attached PDF.

blocks.description: Slack Block Kit blocks. See https://api.slack.com/block-kit. Each block is a {type, text, fields, accessory, ...} object.

username.description: Override the display name for this message (the webhook's default is set in Slack's admin UI).

thread_ts.description: Reply in an existing thread by parent timestamp. New top-level message when omitted.

404.description: no_service — webhook URL is invalid or revoked. Regenerate in Slack admin.
